creator: riggs@rrr.de
text:
I don't have an RV40 file lying around, so I used these demo streams:
http://www.realnetworks.com/products/codecs/realvideo.html

Mplayer detects them as

REAL file format detected.
Stream description: Video Stream
Stream mimetype: video/x-pn-realvideo
[real] Video stream found, -vid 0
Stream description: Audio Stream
Stream mimetype: audio/x-pn-realaudio
[real] Audio stream found, -aid 1
Stream mimetype: logical-fileinfo
VIDEO:  [RV40]  480x232  24bpp  30.000 fps    0.0 kbps ( 0.0 kbyte/s)

So far not unlike your file.
However, it plays flawlessly,

Opening video decoder: [realvid] RealVideo decoder
Selected video codec: [rv3040] vfm: realvid (Linux RealPlayer 10 RV30/40 decoder)

and so on. No crash, crashing, no errors.
From your log, I see two possible problems. First, right after the
beginning, your mplayer says
"This codecs.conf is too old and incompatible with this MPlayer
release! at line 6"
How does that happen? We don't install a codecs.conf file for ages. Do
you have one in your ~/.mplayer folder?
The other possible problem is of course the message
"Error: Shared object "libstdc++.so.5" not found, required by "drvc.so"
 Error loading dll
 ERROR: Could not open required DirectShow codec drvc.so."

Remember that drvc.so is not a native lib:
~ > file /usr/local/lib/win32/drvc.so
/usr/local/lib/win32/drvc.so: ELF 32-bit LSB shared object, Intel 80386, version 1 (SYSV), stripped

You do need to have the linux ABI module loaded and linux_base
installed. It should provide the necessary libstdc++.

Please check if that helps,

Riggs

-- 
- "[...] I talked to the computer at great length and
-- explained my view of the Universe to it" said Marvin.
--- And what happened?" pressed Ford.
---- "It committed suicide." said Marvin.
